Real "previous → current" version jumps, even for rolling `:latest` images.

## Added
- **ShipLog remembers the running version per container.** A rolling `:latest`
  image is pinned to a real version once its digest matches the newest release,
  then carried forward across checks — so when it later updates you see a true
  **"1.7 → 1.8"** in the bubble instead of just "latest". A pinned tag is always
  its own version.

## Changed
- The bubble header shows the remembered version as the current one.

## Fixed
- The newest version is **proven by digest**, so a `:latest` image that lags a
  newer published tag is never mislabeled as a version it isn't running.
- Store migration: upgrading from an older database no longer aborts the first
  check for pre-existing containers (NULL `running_version` is tolerated).

## Notes
- The "1.7 → 1.8" jump appears after ShipLog has seen at least one update under
  its watch (it learns the running version on the first matching check). Images
  with no upstream version tags still show what's available, without a number.

## Install / update

Unraid → **Plugins**. Coming from a date-versioned test build, remove + reinstall
once (or run the one-time `removepkg shiplog` + `installpkg` shown in v1.0.3).

```
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junkerderprovinz/shiplog/main/plugin/shiplog.plg
```
